text: Forked sweep complete (baseline committed: benchmarks/results/2026-07-10-breeze-baseline.md). Data-backed work list, worst first: (1) AtA 0.12-0.26x — needs a dedicated syrk-style symmetric-rank-k kernel (exploit symmetry, row-major-friendly traversal), currently the single worst pair; (2) gemm/gemmTall 0.37-0.51x — register blocking + micro-kernel unroll-and-jam, retune block=64/64^3 threshold; (3) gemv 0.29-0.50x — unrolled inner loop (gemvT already 0.83-0.92x, shows the traversal that works); (4) dot/axpy 0.29-0.43x at 65k-1M — memory-bound regime, 4-way+ unroll to let the JIT vectorize (F2J does this manually); norm already 3-5x AHEAD (overflow-safe scaling pays for Breeze — do not regress it). Large-n QR (0.26x), least-squares (0.28x), LU/solve (0.83x), eigSym-128 (0.68x) are DOWNSTREAM of gemm/gemv — no separate work needed, re-measure after kernel fixes. gale already ahead: norm, Chol<=64 (2.4-3x), all factorizations at n=16, eigSym<=64.
